brands.name: Angelina Italian Bakery
brands.notes: NYC-based quick-serve Italian bakery (Angelina Bakery) that began franchising in 2023; very new, small system (~9 units, +350% growth claim) with a high and wide investment range. Requires food & beverage experience.

riskFlags.title: Very new, unproven franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2019, only began offering franchises in 2023 with ~9 units; limited franchisee track record.

riskFlags.title: High and very wide investment band
riskFlags.detail: Total investment ranges roughly $455K to $1.79M; high capital requirement and $350K liquid capital minimum for a young system.

nationwide.note: Brand markets nationwide/global expansion; no per-state registration list published. Treat as open generally where lead-gen accepted.

liquidCapitalMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / Angelina official franchising page
liquidCapitalMin.note: Confirmed on both Entrepreneur ($350,000) and angelinabakery.com/franchising ('350K+ to start').

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $454,800-$1,790,000 per Entrepreneur; official site shows similar $439,800-$1,732,500.

totalInvestmentMax.note: Top of range; varies by location/buildout.

franchiseFee.note: Note: some FDD-summary sources cite ~$40,000; Entrepreneur lists $50,000.

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur shows +350% unit growth, but from a tiny base.

brands.name: Beef-a-Roo
brands.notes: 58-year-old Rockford, IL-based burger/roast beef QSR (owned by Elysian Capital since 2019) that launched franchising in 2022. In April 2026 eight company/area Rockford locations abruptly closed amid an Illinois Dept. of Revenue registration lapse and unpaid-vendor/bounced-check claims; company said most would reopen. Financial-distress signals are material.

riskFlags.title: Sudden multi-location closure & state tax registration lapse (April 2026)
riskFlags.detail: Eight Rockford-area Beef-A-Roo locations closed April 27, 2026 with Illinois Dept. of Revenue notices that the certificate of registration had expired; company attributed it to finance leadership changes and a banking transition, promising reopening ~April 28.

riskFlags.title: Unpaid vendor and bounced-check claims
riskFlags.detail: Vendor owed $22,700 for snow removal (Dec 2025-Apr 2026) and multiple school PTOs reported bounced fundraising checks; signals cash-flow/financial-control problems.

riskFlags.title: Landlord eviction/unpaid-rent dispute (~$456K)
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Winnebago County eviction proceedings claimed Beef-a-Roo owed $455,823.59 in past-due rent across seven area locations.

riskFlags.title: Newer franchise program
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only launched in 2022; small franchised footprint and 2025+ 'first nationwide' push.

statesOpen.note: Home market (Rockford-area, Freeport, Elgin) plus franchising.
First out-of-state franchise (Rose City, MI) opened 2023.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Lowest model (conversion) starts ~$479,200; drive-thru and traditional models run higher. Official site markets a narrower $795K-$1.5M band.

totalUnits.note: Company states ~29 restaurants nationwide; mix of legacy company and franchised units. Note 8 Rockford-area units closed temporarily April 2026.

yearFounded.note: Implied from '58 Years of Flavor' marketing in 2025.

isGrowing.note: Recent closures and distress signals contradict a growth narrative; flagged rather than asserted as growing.

brands.name: Boss' Pizza and Chicken
brands.notes: Sioux Falls, SD-founded (2005) pizza, wings and broasted chicken concept operating in SD/ND/NE/IA; founder sold his Sioux Falls restaurants to focus on franchising, which began in 2021. Small but functioning system (~12 units).

riskFlags.title: Small, newer franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2021 with ~12 total locations concentrated in the Upper Midwest; limited geographic and franchisee track record.

riskFlags.title: Founder divested operating restaurants to focus on franchising
riskFlags.detail: Founder sold his Sioux Falls company restaurants to concentrate on the franchising business; a strategic shift rather than a red flag, noted for transparency.

statesOpen.note: Home state; multiple locations (Sioux Falls, Rapid City, Keystone, Yankton).
Listed service area on franchise contact page.
Listed service area on franchise contact page.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Reported as average yearly gross sales per outlet from FDD-summary analysis; SharpSheets indicates this reflects system averages and may not be an explicit Item 19 disclosure. Treat as indicative only.

brands.name: Bowled Healthy Food Company
brands.notes: Healthy fast-casual bowls/no-cook fresh-ingredient concept (Bowled Co.) founded 2020, began franchising 2022. Small but active system (~8-9 locations, mostly upstate New York plus one South Carolina). Low buildout cost positioning.

riskFlags.title: Very new, small franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2020 and only began franchising in 2022; ~8-9 units concentrated in upstate NY with one SC location. Limited multi-market validation.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / Bowled Co. franchise page

statesOpen.note: Majority of locations in upstate NY (Albany/Delmar, Schenectady, Scotia, Malta, Troy, Latham, Amsterdam).

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $116,500-$273,000 per Entrepreneur; official brochure cites ~$123,250 minimum including fee.

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / Bowled Co. official site

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur lists 9; official site enumerates ~8 current locations. Small system.

isGrowing.note: Brand markets 'expanding fast'; from a small base.

brands.name: Brooklyn Robot Foundry
brands.notes: STEAM/robotics and engineering classes and workshops for kids (and adults) founded 2011; began franchising March 2022. Low-investment education concept (~$91K-$146K), ~16 units, current 2025 FDD on file.

riskFlags.title: Newer franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Concept founded 2011 but only began franchising in March 2022; ~16 units and limited mature-franchisee history.

nationwide.note: Brand markets franchise availability across multiple U.S. regions without a published per-state registration list; treat as broadly open subject to state registration.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum liquid capital $30,000; minimum net worth $350,000 per FDD summary.

franchiseFee.note: Entrepreneur lists $38,500; some FDD summaries reference a $40,000 figure.

fddAvailable.note: Current 2025 FDD is on file/publicly indexed.
